name: Current Affairs in Geography of India (10): The National River Linking Project's Status (2026)

sourceRefs.label: hakaimagazine.com/jalshakti-dowr.gov.in - National River Linking Project scope (30 links, $168bn); 2026 implementation status (PFRs/FRs/DPRs); 2026 biodiversity-risk research

researchNotes: Twelfth Geography current-affairs topic (10th lap). Directly extends the Day-61 Ken-Betwa Link Project topic with the BROADER National River Linking Project framework it belongs to — connecting an already-covered specific project to its larger national context. Expansion pass (Batch 22): added the National Perspective Plan's 1980 origin and Himalayan/Peninsular component split.

reading.intro: Covers India's National River Linking Project (NRLP) — its full scope, 2026 implementation status, and emerging environmental research concerns.

qa.question: What is the full scope of India's National River Linking Project (NRLP)?
qa.answer: 30 planned river links, intended to transfer an estimated 200 billion cubic metres of water around the country annually, at an estimated cost of about $168 billion.

qa.question: Which specific link project (already covered as a standalone topic) is the FIRST to enter implementation under the NRLP framework?
qa.answer: The Ken-Betwa Link Project — its foundation stone was laid in December 2024, diverting surplus water from the Ken river to the Betwa river.

qa.question: What is the NRLP's 2026 documentation/planning status across all 30 projects?
qa.answer: Pre-Feasibility Reports (PFRs): completed for ALL 30 projects. Feasibility Reports (FRs): completed for 26 projects. Detailed Project Reports (DPRs): completed for 11 projects.

qa.question: What environmental concerns did 2026 research raise about river interlinking?
qa.answer: That the interlinking routes could act as ecological corridors enabling biotic exchange between previously-isolated river basins — potentially accelerating biological invasions, disrupting migratory routes, altering hydrological regimes, and undermining ecosystem resilience.

qa.question: What principle governs the implementation of interlinking-of-rivers (ILR) projects across states?
qa.answer: CONSENSUS — implementation depends on agreement among the concerned states, since river-linking often involves inter-state water-sharing.

qa.question: Which body originally conceptualised India's National Perspective Plan (NPP) for river interlinking, and when?
qa.answer: Prepared by the Ministry of Water Resources in 1980, dividing the NRLP into the Himalayan Component (14 links) and the Peninsular Component (16 links) — the same 30-link structure still guiding the programme today.

questions.explanation: 1980, by the Ministry of Water Resources.
